source: Compiled from QS Rankings, institutional data, UCAS, Study.com

keyDifferences: Apply for a specific subject from day 1
topDestinations.admissions: Strong predicted grades (A*A*A typical for Oxbridge), personal statement focused on academic interest, subject-specific admissions tests (MAT, BMAT, TSA, LNAT)
topDestinations.financialAid: Limited for international students. Scholarships: Chevening, Commonwealth, Gates Cambridge, Rhodes (postgrad). Some universities offer merit awards.
topDestinations.visaTips: Student visa (Tier 4) required. Can work 20hrs/week during term. Post-study work visa: 2 years (Graduate Route).

topDestinations.system: Direct to each university (no centralized system except Ontario OUAC)
keyDifferences: Co-op programs very common (Waterloo is king)
More affordable than US/UK for comparable quality
Path to permanent residency via PGWP
topDestinations.admissions: Grades-focused (less holistic than US). SAT/ACT optional at most schools. Some programs require supplementary applications (Waterloo AIF, UBC Broad-Based Admissions).
topDestinations.financialAid: International student scholarships available at most major universities. Less generous than US need-based aid. Merit-based entrance scholarships common ($3K-$40K).
topDestinations.visaTips: Study permit required. Can work 20hrs/week. PGWP (Post-Graduation Work Permit) for up to 3 years → path to PR via Express Entry.

topDestinations.system: Direct to each university; UAC (NSW), VTAC (Victoria)
keyDifferences: Group of Eight (Go8) is the top tier
Strong in STEM, health sciences, business
topDestinations.financialAid: Australia Awards (government scholarships), Destination Australia, university-specific scholarships. Generally less aid available than US.
topDestinations.visaTips: Student visa (subclass 500). Post-study work visa: 2-4 years depending on qualification level.

highlights.tuition: Free or near-free at public universities (even for internationals)
highlights.language: Growing number of English-taught programs, especially at masters level

highlights.note: ETH Zurich and EPFL are world-class STEM at incredible value

visaTypes.F1: Standard student visa. Can work on-campus 20hrs/week. OPT: 12 months post-graduation (36 months for STEM).
visaTypes.J1: Exchange visitor. More restrictive. 2-year home residency requirement may apply.

financialAidForInternational.meritScholarships: Many T50-T100 schools offer merit scholarships to international students to attract talent
financialAidForInternational.strategy: Apply to a mix of need-blind, need-aware generous, and merit-focused schools. Run NPCs where available.

admissionsTips: TOEFL/IELTS required unless educated in English for 4+ years
International applicants often evaluated in regional pools
Demonstrated interest matters more at some schools
Financial documentation (bank statements) required early in process
Some schools have higher acceptance rates for international students; others are more selective
